summary: Legacy validated record without machine-checkable validation proof. Official Windows service control for Bluetooth Audio Gateway Service (BTAGService). Microsoft documents the service name, default start mode, and says it is OK to disable. The app disables the service by setting its start mode to Disabled.

setting.name: Bluetooth Audio Gateway Service
setting.casual_explanation: This service supports Bluetooth hands-free audio gateway features. Disabling it removes that capability.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable it on systems that never use Bluetooth hands-free audio and want fewer background services.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Disabling it can reduce unused Bluetooth service activity, but Bluetooth audio-gateway features stop working until the service is restored.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft's Windows IoT Enterprise service guidance lists Bluetooth Audio Gateway Service `(BTAGService)` with a default start mode of `Manual` and marks it `OK to disable`. The app uses a service start-mode tweak that disables `BTAGService`.

allowed_values.label: Windows default
allowed_values.meaning: The service starts on demand with Microsoft's documented default mode.

allowed_values.label: Bluetooth audio gateway disabled
allowed_values.meaning: The service no longer starts until its start mode is restored.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app disables BTAGService by setting its start mode to Disabled.

windows_defaults.label: Microsoft documented default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows systems using the normal Bluetooth Audio Gateway service configuration

states.rationale: Microsoft lists BTAGService with a Manual default start mode.

windows_defaults.notes: This baseline preserves the documented Bluetooth audio-gateway behavior.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: People using Bluetooth hands-free audio features
avoid_for: Systems that intentionally do not use Bluetooth audio-gateway features

states.rationale: Keep the documented default service mode.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You use Bluetooth hands-free or related audio-gateway features.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You never use those features and want the service off.
tradeoffs: The service can still start when Windows needs it.

recommended_profiles.label: No Bluetooth audio gateway
intended_for: Systems not using Bluetooth hands-free audio
Users stripping down unused Bluetooth components
avoid_for: Headset or hands-free Bluetooth use cases

states.rationale: Disable the documented Bluetooth Audio Gateway service.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You intentionally do not want Bluetooth audio-gateway support.
tradeoffs: The Bluetooth audio-gateway service stops starting.
Hands-free Bluetooth audio-gateway capability is removed until restored.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: Guidance on configuring system services - Bluetooth Audio Gateway Service
evidence.summary: Microsoft lists Bluetooth Audio Gateway Service `(BTAGService)` with start mode `Manual` and marks it `OK to disable`.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider disables the `BTAGService` service through the batch service start-mode tweak.

decision.why: Microsoft documents the BTAGService service name, its Manual default start mode, and that it is OK to disable in the service guidance used here. The app's service-start-mode action matches that documented control surface.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Bluetooth Audio Gateway Service (BTAGService); Manual; Service supporting the audio gateway role of the Bluetooth Handsfree Profile.; OK to disable: This service provides functionality that is useful to some but not all enterprises, and security-focused enterprises that don't use it can safely disable it.
validation_proof.notes: The Microsoft services guidance page explicitly lists the BTAGService service, its Manual baseline, its Bluetooth Handsfree Profile role, and that it is OK to disable.
